description: Letter from Nissim b. Yiṣḥaq al-Tāhartī, in Sūsa, to Nahray b. Nissim, in Fustat. Dating: ca. 1052 CE. The letter mentions the connections that Nahray has with the Tāhartī family as well as business matters with two Muslims, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān and his son Abū ʿAbdallāh (Ibn al-Baʿbāʿ, r I. 39 and margin). It mentions also Nissim b. Ya'aqov, who leads a center of learning in Qayrawān. The writer respects Nahray and will try to get him the books that he wants. Nissim opens the letter by conveying his concern for Nahray's eye disease, and his happiness that Nahray's condition is better now than "when the doctors frightened you regarding it" (r4–6). Later, in the context of Nissim's troubles this winter, he conveys the news of a woman (identity unclear) who is very sick (recto, right margin). (Information in part from Gil, Kingdom, 3, pp. 319–24, #389)
tags: illness letter 969-1517, Ibn al-Ba'ba', Illness: physician, Illness: women's, Illness, Nahray B. Nissim, 11th c, al-Tahirti, trade, illness: eye, illness: fright
description_he: מכתב מנסים בן יצחק אל-טאהרתי, בסוסה, לנהריי בן נסים, פוסטאט, כ-1052. עסקים עם שני מוסלמים; מזכיר את נסים בן יעקב בקירואן. מביע דאגה למחלת העיניים של נהריי ושמחה על שיפור מצבו.
